name: Barbecued Chicken
recipeIngredient: 4 Skinless Boneless Chicken Breasts (4 oz. or more each)
1/2 Tablespoon White Horseradish, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler. Heat charcoal grill until it forms a white ash, or preheat a gas grill to medium.
2. In a small saucepan, combine ketchup, vinegar, horseradish, brown sugar, garlic, and thyme. Mix well.
3. Bring mixture to a boil over medium-low heat. Cook, stirring frequently, until thickened, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in pepper.
4. Brush tops of chicken pieces lightly with sauce. Place chicken, sauce-side down, on a foil-lined broiler pan or grill rack.
5. Brush the other sides lightly with sauce.
6. Broil or grill 3 inches from the heat, basting with remaining sauce and turning until no longer pink in the center, about 5 to 7 minutes per side (my chicken took longer because of its thickness and distance from the heat).
7. Let chicken stand for 5 minutes before serving. Serve with rice (I used Rice Roni), tossed green salad, and mixed vegetables.
